skill.name: jstodocx-api
skill.description: Generate Microsoft Word DOCX files through the jstodocx.com API from basic HTML, JavaScript source text, or structured template fields.
skill.summary: Create DOCX files from HTML, JavaScript source, or templates through an external API.
skill.author: daanaagua

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command, network, environment, and weak-crypto hits. Review found no prompt injection, malicious code, or weak cryptography; most command hits are markdown examples. The skill intentionally sends user-provided document content to jstodocx.com and documents API key headers, so it should publish with a network and credential-handling warning.

medium_findings.title: External API Receives User Document Content
medium_findings.description: The workflow sends document content to POST https://jstodocx.com/api/v1/docx. This is intended behavior for the skill, but it can expose confidential user content to a third-party service if used without consent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly define an external POST endpoint and document request bodies containing HTML or template fields. The risk is data exposure through intended network behavior, not hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: API Key Handling Requires Care
medium_findings.description: The documentation supports Authorization Bearer and x-api-key headers, plus a shell example using $JSTODOCX_API_KEY. The text warns not to print or store keys, but users could still leak credentials through shell history, logs, or copied commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Credential headers and an environment variable example are present, and the safety guidance reduces but does not remove misuse risk. No evidence shows the skill attempts to steal keys.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Trigger Static Command Detections
low_findings.description: The command detections are in documentation examples for curl, file, unzip, and head. These are bounded examples for API use and DOCX validation, not hidden execution logic inside the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious patterns appear inside fenced markdown examples and use fixed commands. No source file contains executable script logic that dynamically runs user input.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain descriptions, API response text, or headings. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic implementation in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found only prose and API documentation. There is no cryptographic algorithm selection or implementation at those locations.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Hits Are Benign Validation Notes
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged status-code and file-inspection documentation. These lines describe HTTP errors and optional DOCX validation, not host reconnaissance or broad system inventory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited section is a short error and validation reference for generated files. It does not instruct collection of machine identity, environment details, or sensitive host data.

content.user_title: Generate Word Documents with jstodocx API
content.value_statement: Creating DOCX files from agent workflows often requires extra libraries or manual export steps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a lightweight API workflow for HTML, source archives, and template documents.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to call the jstodocx.com DOCX endpoint with JSON requests.
Supports basic HTML conversion for headings, paragraphs, lists, tables, and inline formatting.
Formats JavaScript source text into a Word document without executing the code.
Documents template mode for reports, invoices, proposals, resumes, and meeting notes.
Lists expected response headers, status codes, and basic DOCX validation checks.
Provides safety guidance for API keys, confidential content, and logging.
limitations: Requires access to the external jstodocx.com service and may require an API key.
Does not promise pixel-perfect conversion from complex HTML or browser layouts.
Should not be used with confidential content unless the user approves external processing.
Only documents the API workflow; it does not include a local DOCX generation library.

use_cases.title: Export release notes
use_cases.description: Convert a simple HTML release summary into a Word document for business review.

use_cases.title: Archive JavaScript snippets
use_cases.description: Place source text in a readable DOCX with optional line numbers for handoff or review.

use_cases.title: Draft structured business documents
use_cases.description: Generate invoices, proposals, reports, resumes, or meeting notes from controlled fields.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic DOCX
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with simple HTML content.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use jstodocx-api to create a DOCX named release-plan.docx from this basic HTML: [paste HTML]. Confirm before sending any confidential content.

prompt_templates.title: Export source text
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for code archival without execution.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use jstodocx-api in js-source mode to turn this JavaScript source into a DOCX with line numbers. Treat the code as text only.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a template document
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured business document from known fields.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use jstodocx-api template mode to create a proposal DOCX from these fields: heading, paragraph, bullets, rows, and footer.

prompt_templates.title: Integrate API workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a safe script workflow for calling jstodocx-api, handling 401 and 429 responses, saving the DOCX, and avoiding API key logs.

output_examples.input: A user provides a short HTML release plan and asks for a Word file.
output_examples.output: A saved DOCX file with the requested filename, basic headings, paragraphs, lists, and a confirmation of response status.

output_examples.input: A user supplies JavaScript source and asks for a document archive.
output_examples.output: A Word document containing formatted source text with optional line numbers and no code execution.

output_examples.input: A user provides invoice fields such as heading, line items, terms, and footer.
output_examples.output: A generated invoice DOCX using the supported template fields and a concise completion summary.

best_practices: Ask before sending confidential or regulated content to the external API.
Pass API keys through environment variables or secure secret storage, not pasted logs.
Validate the saved response by checking content type, filename, and DOCX archive structure.
anti_patterns: Do not use js-source mode to execute or evaluate JavaScript code.
Do not send protected documents to the API without clear user approval.
Do not claim exact browser rendering or full HTML compatibility for generated DOCX files.

faq.question: Does this skill create DOCX files locally?
faq.answer: No. It documents an external API workflow for creating DOCX files through jstodocx.com.

faq.question: Can it convert any HTML page exactly?
faq.answer: No. It supports basic document HTML and does not promise pixel-perfect browser rendering.

faq.question: Does js-source mode run JavaScript?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that JavaScript is treated as source text for document formatting.

faq.question: Is an API key required?
faq.answer: The reference says the endpoint may be public or may require a key, depending on site configuration.

faq.question: What document templates are listed?
faq.answer: The reference lists report, invoice, proposal, resume, and meeting-notes templates.

faq.question: What should users avoid sending?
faq.answer: Users should avoid protected or confidential content unless they approve sending it to the external API.
